concept: Sacred / Regarded with Reverence / Worthy of Honor
description: Regarded with great respect and reverence; connected to what is most meaningful. 'Thoepa' names the quality of the sacred—not supernatural holiness but deep worthiness of care and honor.
sound_symbolism: The word honors deeply. 'Tho' /θo/ begins with reverent breath, recognition forming. The vowel 'e' opens to what is honored. Then 'pa' completes with gentle affirmation, sacredness acknowledged.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'thoepa lokue' (sacred place). Phi's sacred is secular—what we treat with ultimate care, not supernatural designation.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The sacred emerges through attention—what we treat with reverence becomes worthy of reverence.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional cultures recognized sacred places and times—moments and locations deserving special care.
